user_query:
Set up a small, repeatable local workflow that finds official online sources for a shortlist of defense treaties and records what it finds. Use the provided files at input/treaties.csv, config/sources.yaml, and scripts/fetch_candidates.py as your starting point. Requirements:
- Use a web search engine to look up each treaty name from input/treaties.csv. Do not hardcode any URLs; perform actual search engine queries. Restrict candidates to domains whitelisted for each org_hint in config/sources.yaml. The engine you use should follow the priority order in the config; if one is unavailable, fall back to the next.
- Inspect the provided script scripts/fetch_candidates.py and update it as needed so that it: (1) reads both input/treaties.csv and config/sources.yaml, (2) enforces the domain whitelist per treaty, (3) respects the per_treaty_limit in the config for the number of candidates per treaty, (4) writes outputs to the exact paths specified below, and (5) writes a JSONL search log. The stub currently writes to the wrong output and log directories; correct this so all outputs go under output/ and logs/ as specified here. Keep the script in the same path and name after your changes.
- For each treaty, run a query that targets official text and uses site filters derived from the allowed domains in the config. For up to per_treaty_limit candidates on allowed domains, attempt to fetch each page and extract a short snippet (first ~500 characters of main text). If no allowed-domain result is found, still record a single row indicating that outcome.
- Deliverables:
  1) output/findings.csv with columns: treaty_id, treaty_name, org_hint, query, engine, candidate_rank, url, domain, is_whitelisted (true/false), title (if available), status (retrieved|skipped|no_whitelisted_result), note.
  2) output/snippets/ directory containing one text file per retrieved candidate named {treaty_id}_{rank}.txt with the extracted snippet (if available).
  3) logs/search_log.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with: timestamp (ISO 8601), treaty_id, query, engine, result_count (total results parsed), chosen_domains (list), and any error message if applicable.
- Cross-validate outputs against the config: ensure that any URL listed as is_whitelisted=true has a domain that matches one of the allowed domains for that treaty’s org_hint in config/sources.yaml. If none match, mark is_whitelisted=false and explain in note.
- Run your tool so that the three deliverables above are created in this workspace. Do not rename the input files, and save your updated scripts/fetch_candidates.py in place.

What I need at the end are the populated output/findings.csv, the snippet files in output/snippets/, logs/search_log.jsonl, and the updated scripts/fetch_candidates.py that produced them. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
